### Install Project Dependencies with Yarn

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to install all necessary project dependencies using Yarn. Run this after cloning the repository to prepare the development environment.

```sh
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod Mini

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Install Zod version 4.0.0 or higher to access the Zod Mini package.

```shell
npm install zod@^4.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Zod Repository and Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Initial steps to set up the Zod project for local development, including cloning the repository and installing necessary packages.

```bash
git clone git@github.com:{your_username}/zod.git
```

```bash
cd zod
```

```bash
pnpm i
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod via npm (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/index.mdx

Use this command to install the Zod library as a dependency in your project using npm.

```sh
npm install zod
```

--------------------------------

### Run Zod Docs Development Server

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to start a local development server for the Zod documentation, enabling real-time preview of changes.

```bash
pnpm run --filter=@zod/docs dev
```

--------------------------------

### Making a schema optional

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Shows how to make a schema optional, allowing 'undefined' inputs, with examples for both Zod and Zod Mini.

```ts
z.optional(z.literal("yoda")); // or z.literal("yoda").optional()
```

```ts
z.optional(z.literal("yoda"));
```

--------------------------------

### Registering a Zod Mini Schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Example of using the `.register()` method to add a Zod Mini schema to a custom registry, associating it with metadata.

```ts
const myReg = z.registry<{title: string}>();

z.string().register(myReg, { title: "My cool string schema" });
```

--------------------------------

### New Zod Transform Method

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/MIGRATION.md

This example demonstrates the updated way to apply transformations in Zod 3. The `.transform()` method is now available directly on all Zod schemas.

```ts
z.string().transform((val) => val.length);
```

--------------------------------

### Perform a Zod Project Release

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/AGENTS.md

Execute the necessary Git commands to bump versions and push changes to trigger a new release. Ensure `main` is clean and up to date before starting.

```bash
# Make sure main is clean and up to date first.
git checkout main && git pull

# Bump all three files to the new x.y.z, then:
git add packages/zod/package.json packages/zod/jsr.json packages/zod/src/v4/core/versions.ts
git commit -m "<x.y.z>"   # commit message is just the version, e.g. "4.4.3"
git push origin main
```

--------------------------------

### Example output of `z.prettifyError()`

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/error-formatting.mdx

The formatted string output produced by `z.prettifyError()`.

```text
✖ Unrecognized key: "extraKey"
✖ Invalid input: expected string, received number
  → at username
✖ Invalid input: expected number, received string
  → at favoriteNumbers[1]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod Stable Version

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Install the latest stable version of Zod using your preferred package manager. This provides access to Zod's core functionalities.

```sh
npm install zod
```

```sh
deno add npm:zod
```

```sh
yarn add zod
```

```sh
bun add zod
```

```sh
pnpm add zod
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod Canary Version

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Install the canary version of Zod to access the latest features and bug fixes. Canary versions are published on every commit.

```sh
npm install zod@canary
```

```sh
deno add npm:zod@canary
```

```sh
yarn add zod@canary
```

```sh
bun add zod@canary
```

```sh
pnpm add zod@canary
```

--------------------------------

### Normalize URLs using new URL().href

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

This example demonstrates how new URL().href normalizes a URL, which is relevant when using the normalize flag in Zod's z.url().

```ts
new URL("HTTP://ExAmPle.com:80/./a/../b?X=1#f oo").href
// => "http://example.com/b?X=1#f%20oo"
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript Structural Typing Example

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Illustrates TypeScript's default structural typing behavior where types with identical structures are considered compatible.

```ts
type Cat = { name: string };
type Dog = { name: string };

const pluto: Dog = { name: "pluto" };
const simba: Cat = pluto; // works fine
```

--------------------------------

### Demonstrate String Coercion with Various Inputs in Zod

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This example shows how `z.coerce.string()` handles different input types, including numbers, booleans, undefined, and null, by converting them to their string representations.

```ts
schema.parse(12); // => "12"
schema.parse(true); // => "true"
schema.parse(undefined); // => "undefined"
schema.parse(null); // => "null"
```

--------------------------------

### Define a Recipe object schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This schema defines a `Recipe` object with `id`, `name`, and `ingredients` properties, used as a base for `.pick` and `.omit` examples.

```ts
const Recipe = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  ingredients: z.array(z.string())
});
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Data Transformation with Transform

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Use the `transform` method to modify data after it has been successfully parsed by the schema. This example converts a string to its length.

```ts
const stringToNumber = z.string().transform((val) => val.length);

stringToNumber.parse("string"); // => 6
```

--------------------------------

### Define a Base Zod Object Schema for Recipes

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

This schema defines the structure for a recipe, including title, optional description, and an array of ingredients. It serves as a base for `pick` and `omit` examples.

```ts
const Recipe = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string().optional(),
  ingredients: z.array(z.string()),
});
// { title: string; description?: string | undefined; ingredients: string[] }
```

--------------------------------

### Parse boolean with Zod Mini

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Simple example demonstrating parsing a boolean value with Zod Mini. This minimal script shows a 64% bundle size reduction compared to regular Zod.

```typescript
z.boolean().parse(true)
```

--------------------------------

### Structural typing example in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Demonstrates how TypeScript's structural type system treats structurally equivalent types as the same, allowing incompatible values to be passed to functions.

```typescript
type Cat = { name: string };
type Dog = { name: string };

const petCat = (cat: Cat) => {};
const fido: Dog = { name: "fido" };
petCat(fido); // works fine
```

--------------------------------

### Build Zod Docs for Production

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Steps to generate a production build of the Zod documentation, requiring a GitHub Personal Access Token for fetching stargazer counts.

```bash
export GITHUB_TOKEN=your_token_here
```

```bash
pnpm run --filter=@zod/docs build
```

--------------------------------

### Run Zod Play File for Experimentation

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to execute the 'play.ts' file and watch for changes, facilitating quick experimentation during development.

```bash
pnpm dev:play
```

--------------------------------

### Set Up a Git Worktree for PR Iteration

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/AGENTS.md

Create and initialize a Git worktree to iterate on a contributor's pull request without affecting the main branch. This allows for isolated development and testing.

```bash
# 1. Fetch the PR as a local branch and create a worktree for it
git fetch origin pull/<N>/head:pr-<N>
git worktree add ~/.cursor/worktrees/zod/pr-<N> pr-<N>
cd ~/.cursor/worktrees/zod/pr-<N>
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile   # fast, pnpm store is shared across worktrees

# 2. Look up the PR's head info — you'll need the contributor's fork URL
```

--------------------------------

### Example Output of Zod Validation Issues

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/ERROR_HANDLING.md

This snippet shows the structure of the `ZodIssue` array returned when the previous parsing example fails, detailing different error codes, expected/received types, and paths to the invalid data.

```ts
[
  {
    code: "invalid_type",
    expected: "string",
    received: "number",
    path: ["names", 1],
    message: "Invalid input: expected string, received number",
  },
  {
    code: "unrecognized_keys",
    keys: ["extra"],
    path: ["address"],
    message: "Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'extra'",
  },
  {
    code: "too_small",
    minimum: 10000,
    type: "number",
    inclusive: true,
    path: ["address", "zipCode"],
    message: "Value should be greater than or equal to 10000",
  }
];
```

--------------------------------

### Apply metadata using `.meta()` for global registry

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Use the `.meta()` method as a convenient shorthand to register metadata for a schema in `z.globalRegistry`. The syntax varies slightly between Zod and Zod Mini.

```ts
const emailSchema = z.email().meta({ 
  id: "email_address",
  title: "Email address",
  description: "Please enter a valid email address",
});
```

```ts
const emailSchema = z.email().check(
  z.meta({ 
    id: "email_address",
    title: "Email address",
    description: "Please enter a valid email address",
  })
);
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve enum options as tuple with .options

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Use the .options property to get the list of allowed values as a tuple.

```typescript
FishEnum.options; // ["Salmon", "Tuna", "Trout"];
```

--------------------------------

### Register schema metadata with `z.globalRegistry`

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Demonstrates how to register a schema with the global registry, providing standard metadata fields like `id`, `title`, and `description`.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

const emailSchema = z.email().register(z.globalRegistry, { 
  id: "email_address",
  title: "Email address",
  description: "Your email address",
  examples: ["first.last@example.com"]
});
```

--------------------------------

### Using .describe() and .meta() for schema descriptions

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

The .describe() method provides a shorthand for adding a description to a schema. The .meta() method is the recommended approach for registering metadata, offering more flexibility.

```ts
const emailSchema = z.email();
emailSchema.describe("An email address");

// equivalent to
emailSchema.meta({ description: "An email address" });
```

```ts
const emailSchema = z.email().check(z.describe("An email address"));

// equivalent to
z.email().check(z.meta({ description: "An email address" }));
```

--------------------------------

### Define a user object schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This schema defines a `user` object with required `email` and `username` properties, used as a base for `.partial` examples.

```ts
const user = z.object({
  email: z.string(),
  username: z.string()
});
// { email: string; username: string }
```

--------------------------------

### Build Zod Project

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to delete the `lib` directory and re-compile `src` to `lib`. Use this to build the project after making changes.

```sh
yarn build
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Docsify in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/index.html

Sets up Docsify documentation site parameters, including navigation levels, auto-scrolling, repository link, router mode, and lists of cross-origin and no-compile links.

```javascript
window.$docsify = {
  subMaxLevel: 1,
  maxLevel: 3,
  auto2top: true,
  repo: "colinhacks/zod",
  routerMode: "history",
  crossOriginLinks: [
    "https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/actions?query=branch%3Amain",
    "https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT",
    "https://www.npmjs.com/package/zod",
    "https://discord.gg/KaSRdyX2vc",
    "https://discord.gg/RcG33DQJdf",
    "https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/new",
    "https://twitter.com/colinhacks",
    "https://trpc.io/",
    "https://zod.dev/",
    "https://deno.land/x/zod",
    "https://zod.dev/blog/clerk-fellowship",
    "https://go.clerk.com/hqN4rp7",
    "https://go.clerk.com/Qgor5NQ",
    "https://go.clerk.com/PKHrcwh"
  ],
  noCompileLinks: [
    "https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/actions?query=branch%3Amain",
    "https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT",
    "https://www.npmjs.com/package/zod",
    "https://discord.gg/KaSRdyX2vc",
    "https://discord.gg/RcG33DQJdf",
    "https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/new",
    "https://twitter.com/colinhacks",
    "https://trpc.io/",
    "https://zod.dev/",
    "https://deno.land/x/zod",
    "https://zod.dev/blog/clerk-fellowship",
    "https://go.clerk.com/hqN4rp7",
    "https://go.clerk.com/Qgor5NQ",
    "https://go.clerk.com/PKHrcwh"
  ]
};
```

--------------------------------

### Define a Zod Object Schema (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/README.md

This example shows how to define a basic object schema using Zod, specifying types for its properties.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

const Player = z.object({
  username: z.string(),
  xp: z.number()
});
```

--------------------------------

### Using Zod Mini's Parsing Methods

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Demonstrates the `parse`, `parseAsync`, `safeParse`, and `safeParseAsync` methods available on Zod Mini schemas, similar to standard Zod.

```ts
import * as z from "zod/mini"

const mySchema = z.string();

mySchema.parse('asdf')
await mySchema.parseAsync('asdf')
mySchema.safeParse('asdf')
await mySchema.safeParseAsync('asdf')
```

--------------------------------

### Access enum values with .enum property

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Use the .enum property on a Zod enum schema to get an object with enum values for autocompletion.

```typescript
FishEnum.enum.Salmon; // => autocompletes

FishEnum.enum;
/*
=> {
  Salmon: "Salmon",
  Tuna: "Tuna",
  Trout: "Trout",
}
*/
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Refinements to Zod Codecs

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Shows how to apply a refinement to a codec, demonstrating that refinements are executed during both `encode()` and `decode()` operations.

```ts
const schema = stringToDate.refine((date) => date.getFullYear() >= 2000, "Must be this millennium");

schema.encode(new Date("2000-01-01"));
// => Date

schema.encode(new Date("1999-01-01"));
// => ❌ ZodError: [
//   {
//     "code": "custom",
//     "path": [],
//     "message": "Must be this millennium"
//   }
// ]
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Zod Validation Errors Asynchronously with .safeParseAsync() (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/README.md

For schemas with asynchronous operations, use `.safeParseAsync()` to get a promise resolving to a result object.

```ts
const schema = z.string().refine(async (val) => val.length <= 8);

await schema.safeParseAsync("hello");
// => { success: true; data: "hello" }
```

--------------------------------

### ZodError Output with Custom Path

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This example shows the structure of the `ZodError` object when a custom `path` is provided in a `refine` method, indicating where the validation issue occurred.

```ts
ZodError {
  issues: [{
    "code": "custom",
    "path": [ "confirm" ],
    "message": "Passwords don't match"
  }]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configuring z.stringbool() for case sensitivity

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Demonstrates how to make z.stringbool() case-sensitive by setting the 'case' option.

```ts
z.stringbool({
  case: "sensitive"
});
```

--------------------------------

### Cloning a Zod Mini Schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Shows how to create an identical copy of an existing Zod Mini schema using the `.clone()` method, passing its internal definition.

```ts
const mySchema = z.string()

mySchema.clone(mySchema._zod.def);
```

--------------------------------

### Define and Use Basic Zod Transforms

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Demonstrates how to create a basic transform that casts any input value to a string and then parses various types.

```ts
const castToString = z.transform((val) => String(val));

castToString.parse("asdf"); // => "asdf"
castToString.parse(123); // => "123"
castToString.parse(true); // => "true"
```

```ts
const castToString = z.transform((val) => String(val));

z.parse(castToString, "asdf"); // => "asdf"
z.parse(castToString, 123); // => "123"
z.parse(castToString, true); // => "true"
```

--------------------------------

### Run Zod Vitest Tests in Watch Mode

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to start Vitest tests in watch mode, automatically re-running tests upon file changes.

```bash
pnpm test:watch
```

--------------------------------

### Constrain schema to specific subclass

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/library-authors.mdx

Use a generic constraint that extends a specific schema subclass to accept only schemas of that type. This example restricts input to object schemas only.

```typescript
import * as z4 from "zod/v4/core";

// only accepts object schemas
function inferSchema<T extends z4.$ZodObject>(schema: T) {
  return schema;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import Zod 3 and Zod 4 Side-by-Side

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/library-authors.mdx

Demonstrates how to import Zod 3 and Zod 4 from their respective subpaths to use them concurrently in a library.

```ts
import * as z3 from "zod/v3";
import * as z4 from "zod/v4/core";

type Schema = z3.ZodTypeAny | z4.$ZodType;

function acceptUserSchema(schema: z3.ZodTypeAny | z4.$ZodType) {
  // ...
}
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Properties of Any Data Type

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

The `z.property()` API can be used with any data type, not just class instances, to validate a specific property. This example validates the 'length' property of a string.

```ts
const blobSchema = z.string().check(
  z.property("length", z.number().min(10))
);

blobSchema.parse("hello there!"); // ✅
blobSchema.parse("hello."); // ❌
```

--------------------------------

### Controlling Refinement Execution with 'when'

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Demonstrates using the `when` parameter to specify conditions under which a refinement should run. This allows the password confirmation to execute even if other fields have errors, by checking the validity of only the relevant fields.

```ts
const baseSchema = z.object({
  password: z.string().min(8),
  confirmPassword: z.string(),
  anotherField: z.string(),
});

const schema = baseSchema
  .refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword, {
    message: "Passwords do not match",
    path: ["confirmPassword"],

    // run if password & confirmPassword are valid
    when(payload) { // [!code ++]
      return baseSchema // [!code ++]
        .pick({ password: true, confirmPassword: true }) // [!code ++]
        .safeParse(payload.value).success; // [!code ++]
    },  // [!code ++]
  });

schema.parse({
  password: "asdf",
  confirmPassword: "asdf",
  anotherField: 1234 // ❌ this error will not prevent the password check from running
});
```

```ts
const schema =
  z.object({
    password: z.string().min(8),
    confirmPassword: z.string(),
    anotherField: z.string(),
  })
  .check(z.refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword, {
    message: "Passwords do not match",
    path: ["confirmPassword"],

    when(payload) { // [!code ++]
      // no issues with `password` or `confirmPassword` // [!code ++]
      return payload.issues.every((iss) => { // [!code ++]
        const firstPathEl = iss.path?.[0]; // [!code ++]
        return firstPathEl !== "password" && firstPathEl !== "confirmPassword"; // [!code ++]
      }); // [!code ++]
    },  // [!code ++]
  }));

schema.parse({
  password: "asdf",
  confirmPassword: "asdf",
  anotherField: 1234 // ❌ this error will prevent the password check from running
});
```

--------------------------------

### Register a schema with metadata using `.register()`

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Use the `.register()` method on a schema to conveniently add it to a specified registry with associated metadata.

```ts
const mySchema = z.string();

mySchema.register(myRegistry, { description: "A cool schema!" });
// => mySchema
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Zod string schema operations

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Demonstrates `parse`, `decode`, and `encode` behavior for a simple `z.string()` schema where input and output types are identical.

```ts
const schema = z.string();

type Input = z.input<typeof schema>;    // string
type Output = z.output<typeof schema>;  // string

schema.parse("asdf");   // => "asdf"
schema.decode("asdf");  // => "asdf"
schema.encode("asdf");  // => "asdf"
```

```ts
const schema = z.string();

type Input = z.input<typeof schema>;    // string
type Output = z.output<typeof schema>;  // string

z.parse(schema, "asdf");   // => "asdf"
z.decode(schema, "asdf");  // => "asdf"
z.encode(schema, "asdf");  // => "asdf"
```

--------------------------------

### Augment `GlobalMeta` interface for custom metadata fields

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Extend the `GlobalMeta` interface using declaration merging to add custom metadata fields, such as `examples`, to the global registry.

```ts
declare module "zod" {
  interface GlobalMeta {
    // add new fields here
    examples?: unknown[];
  }
}

// forces TypeScript to consider the file a module
export {}
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Parse a String Schema with Zod

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This snippet demonstrates how to define a basic string schema and use both `parse` (which throws on error) and `safeParse` (which returns a result object) methods for validation.

```ts
import { z } from "zod";

// creating a schema for strings
const mySchema = z.string();

// parsing
mySchema.parse("tuna"); // => "tuna"
mySchema.parse(12); // => throws ZodError

// "safe" parsing (doesn't throw error if validation fails)
mySchema.safeParse("tuna"); // => { success: true; data: "tuna" }
mySchema.safeParse(12); // => { success: false; error: ZodError }
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Specific Properties of an Instance

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Combine `z.instanceof()` with `z.property()` to validate a specific property of a class instance against a Zod schema. This example checks the 'protocol' property of a URL object.

```ts
const blobSchema = z.instanceof(URL).check(
  z.property("protocol", z.literal("https:" as string, "Only HTTPS allowed"))
);

blobSchema.parse(new URL("https://example.com")); // ✅
blobSchema.parse(new URL("http://example.com")); // ❌
```

--------------------------------

### Define a TypeScript discriminated union type

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

This TypeScript example shows a discriminated union type, where a common property (`status`) allows for type narrowing based on its literal value.

```ts
type MyResult =
  | { status: "success"; data: string }
  | { status: "failed"; error: string };

function handleResult(result: MyResult){
  if(result.status === "success"){
    result.data; // string
  } else {
    result.error; // string
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run All Jest Tests

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to execute all Jest tests and generate a coverage badge. Run this before submitting a PR to ensure no unintended breaking changes.

```sh
yarn test
```

--------------------------------

### Access Inner Schema of Zod Array

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Access the inner schema of a Zod array to get the schema for its elements. Zod uses unwrap(), while Zod Mini uses def.element.

```ts
stringArray.unwrap(); // => string schema
```

```ts
stringArray.def.element; // => string schema
```

--------------------------------

### Import Zod Mini

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Import Zod Mini using the 'zod/mini' path instead of the default 'zod' package.

```typescript
import * as z from "zod/mini";
```

--------------------------------

### Distinguishing Input and Output Types with Zod Transformations (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/README.md

When a Zod schema includes transformations, use `z.input<>` to get the type before transformation and `z.output<>` (or `z.infer<>`) for the type after transformation.

```typescript
const mySchema = z.string().transform((val) => val.length);

type MySchemaIn = z.input<typeof mySchema>;
// => string

type MySchemaOut = z.output<typeof mySchema>; // equivalent to z.infer<typeof mySchema>
// number
```

--------------------------------

### Use Library Function with Zod and Zod Mini Schemas

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/library-authors.mdx

Shows how user code can pass both Zod and Zod Mini schemas to a library function built against `zod/v4/core`.

```ts
// user code
import { acceptObjectSchema } from "your-library";

// Zod 4
import * as z from "zod";
acceptObjectSchema(z.object({ name: z.string() }));

// Zod 4 Mini
import * as zm from "zod/mini";
acceptObjectSchema(zm.object({ name: zm.string() }))
```

--------------------------------

### Importing Zod Core and accessing base classes

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/core.mdx

Imports the core Zod package and demonstrates how to access its fundamental classes for schemas, checks, and errors, along with utility functions.

```typescript
import * as z from "zod/v4/core";

// the base class for all Zod schemas
z.$ZodType;

// subclasses of $ZodType that implement common parsers
z.$ZodString
z.$ZodObject
z.$ZodArray
// ...

// the base class for all Zod checks
z.$ZodCheck;

// subclasses of $ZodCheck that implement common checks
z.$ZodCheckMinLength
z.$ZodCheckMaxLength

// the base class for all Zod errors
z.$ZodError;

// issue formats (types only)
{} as z.$ZodIssue;

// utils
z.util.isValidJWT(...);
```

--------------------------------

### Standard Zod Schema Chaining

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Illustrates how validation methods are chained directly on schema instances in standard Zod.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

z.string()
  .min(5)
  .max(10)
  .refine(val => val.includes("@"))
  .trim()
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Git Push Output

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/AGENTS.md

After an initial push, verify the output to confirm the branch was pushed correctly. If the right-hand side shows 'main', the push was incorrect.

```text
* [new branch]      <branch> -> <branch>
```

--------------------------------

### Define and Infer Type for an Object Schema with Zod

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

This example shows how to create an object schema with Zod and then extract its inferred TypeScript type using `z.infer<typeof Schema>` for type safety.

```ts
import { z } from "zod";

const User = z.object({
  username: z.string()
});

User.parse({ username: "Ludwig" });

// extract the inferred type
type User = z.infer<typeof User>;
// { username: string }
```

--------------------------------

### Parse object schema with Zod Mini

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Example of parsing an object with multiple properties using Zod Mini. This more complex schema demonstrates bundle size benefits when using object types.

```typescript
const schema = z.object({ a: z.string(), b: z.number(), c: z.boolean() });

schema.parse({
  a: "asdf",
  b: 123,
  c: true,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Unwrapping an optional schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Illustrates how to retrieve the original, non-optional schema from an optional instance using library-specific methods.

```ts
optionalYoda.unwrap(); // ZodLiteral<"yoda">
```

```ts
optionalYoda.def.innerType; // ZodMiniLiteral<"yoda">
```

--------------------------------

### Define intersection of Zod union types

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Use `z.intersection()` to create a schema that validates against all provided schemas. This example shows intersecting two union schemas, resulting in a schema that infers to a 'number' type.

```ts
const a = z.union([z.number(), z.string()]);
const b = z.union([z.number(), z.boolean()]);
const c = z.intersection(a, b);

type c = z.infer<typeof c>; // => number
```

--------------------------------

### Create a custom Zod registry with typed metadata

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Initialize a new Zod registry that enforces a specific metadata type, such as an object with a 'description' string.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

const myRegistry = z.registry<{ description: string }>();
```

--------------------------------

### Parse Promise Schemas and Handle Validation

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Demonstrates how parsing works with promise schemas, involving a synchronous check for Promise instance and an asynchronous check for the resolved value. Validation failures for the resolved value must be handled with `.catch()`.

```ts
numberPromise.parse("tuna");
// ZodError: Non-Promise type: string

numberPromise.parse(Promise.resolve("tuna"));
// => Promise<number>

const test = async () => {
  await numberPromise.parse(Promise.resolve("tuna"));
  // ZodError: Non-number type: string

  await numberPromise.parse(Promise.resolve(3.14));
  // => 3.14
};
```

--------------------------------

### Referencing inferred types in custom registry metadata

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Illustrates how to use z.$output within a custom registry's metadata type to include examples that are type-safe with the schema's inferred output.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

type MyMeta = { examples: z.$output[] };
const myRegistry = z.registry<MyMeta>();

myRegistry.add(z.string(), { examples: ["hello", "world"] });
myRegistry.add(z.number(), { examples: [1, 2, 3] });
```

--------------------------------

### Add metadata to Zod schema with .meta()

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/json-schema.mdx

Use `.meta()` to register metadata fields like title and description that get copied into the resulting JSON Schema. In Zod Mini, use `.register()` instead.

```typescript
import * as z from "zod";

// `.meta()` is a convenience method for registering a schema in `z.globalRegistry`
const emailSchema = z.string().meta({ 
  title: "Email address",
  description: "Your email address",
});

z.toJSONSchema(emailSchema);
// => { type: "string", title: "Email address", description: "Your email address", ... }
```

```typescript
import * as z from "zod";

// `.meta()` is a convenience method for registering a schema in `z.globalRegistry`
const emailSchema = z.string().register(z.globalRegistry, { 
  title: "Email address",
  description: "Your email address",
});

z.toJSONSchema(emailSchema);
// => { type: "string", title: "Email address", description: "Your email address", ... }
```

--------------------------------

### Define and Parse a Zod Schema in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/zod/README.md

This snippet demonstrates how to define a basic Zod object schema and parse untrusted input data to get a strongly typed, validated result. It showcases the core functionality of Zod for type-safe data validation.

```ts
import * as z from "zod";

const User = z.object({
  name: z.string(),
});

// some untrusted data...
const input = {
  /* stuff */
};

// the parsed result is validated and type safe!
const data = User.parse(input);

// so you can use it with confidence :)
console.log(data.name);
```

--------------------------------

### Define an Async Zod Codec

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Shows how to create a Zod codec with asynchronous `decode` and `encode` functions, enabling non-blocking transformations.

```ts
const asyncCodec = z.codec(z.string(), z.number(), {
  decode: async (str) => Number(str),
  encode: async (num) => num.toString(),
});
```

--------------------------------

### Compare API styles - String schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Zod Mini uses functional composition with top-level functions instead of method chaining. This enables better tree-shaking but requires wrapping validators in function calls.

```typescript
// regular Zod
const mySchema = z.string().optional().nullable();
```

```typescript
// Zod Mini
const mySchema = z.nullable(z.optional(z.string()));
```

--------------------------------

### Use Async and Safe Variants of Zod decode

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Demonstrates the synchronous, asynchronous, and safe variants of the `decode()` method, including how to handle potential errors and asynchronous operations.

```ts
stringToDate.decode("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); 
// => Date

stringToDate.decodeAsync("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); 
// => Promise<Date>

stringToDate.safeDecode("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); 
// => { success: true, data: Date } | { success: false, error: ZodError }

stringToDate.safeDecodeAsync("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); 
// => Promise<{ success: true, data: Date } | { success: false, error: ZodError }>
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve metadata from a schema using `.meta()`

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/metadata.mdx

Call `.meta()` without arguments on a schema to retrieve its associated metadata from the global registry.

```ts
emailSchema.meta();
// => { id: "email_address", title: "Email address", ... }
```

--------------------------------

### Applying Checks with Zod Mini's `.check()`

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Shows how to apply multiple validation checks to a Zod Mini schema using the `.check()` method, which accepts individual check functions.

```ts
import * as z from "zod/mini"

z.string().check(
  z.minLength(5), 
  z.maxLength(10),
  z.refine(val => val.includes("@")),
  z.trim()
);
```

--------------------------------

### Build Zod with Extended Diagnostics

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/resolution/README.md

Execute this command to build the Zod project using `tsc` with `extendedDiagnostics` enabled, which is useful for performance analysis.

```bash
npm run build-bench
```

--------------------------------

### Branding a Zod Mini Schema

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/mini.mdx

Demonstrates how to use the `.brand()` method to create a branded type for a Zod Mini schema, enhancing type safety.

```ts
import * as z from "zod/mini"

const USD = z.string().brand("USD");
```

--------------------------------

### Overview of ZodType base class methods

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/packages/zod.mdx

Provides a comprehensive list of methods available on all Zod schemas, categorized by parsing, refinements, wrappers, metadata, and utilities.

```typescript
import * as z from "zod";

const mySchema = z.string();

// parsing
mySchema.parse(data);
mySchema.safeParse(data);
mySchema.parseAsync(data);
mySchema.safeParseAsync(data);


// refinements
mySchema.refine(refinementFunc);
mySchema.superRefine(refinementFunc); // deprecated, use `.check()`
mySchema.overwrite(overwriteFunc);

// wrappers
mySchema.optional();
mySchema.nonoptional();
mySchema.nullable();
mySchema.nullish();
mySchema.default(defaultValue);
mySchema.array();
mySchema.or(otherSchema);
mySchema.transform(transformFunc);
mySchema.catch(catchValue);
mySchema.pipe(otherSchema);
mySchema.readonly();

// metadata and registries
mySchema.register(registry, metadata);
mySchema.describe(description);
mySchema.meta(metadata);

// utilities
mySchema.check(checkOrFunction);
mySchema.clone(def);
mySchema.brand<T>();
mySchema.isOptional(); // boolean
mySchema.isNullable(); // boolean
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Random Zod Schemas

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/resolution/README.md

Run this command to generate random Zod schemas for performance testing. Pregenerated schemas are already available in `src/index.ts`.

```bash
node generateRandomSchemas.js
```

--------------------------------

### Codec Utility Functions (z.decode, z.encode, z.invertCodec)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/api.mdx

Provides utility functions to apply codec transformations: `z.decode()` for forward transformation, `z.encode()` for reverse transformation, and `z.invertCodec()` to swap a codec's input/output.

```APIDOC
## z.decode()

### Description
Performs the forward transformation (decoding) using a given codec's `decode` function.

### Signature
`z.decode(codec: ZodCodec, input: any): OutputType`

### Parameters
- **codec** (ZodCodec) - The codec to use for decoding.
- **input** (any) - The input value, expected to conform to the codec's input schema.

### Example
```ts
stringToDate.parse("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); // => Date
z.decode(stringToDate, "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"); // => Date
```

## z.encode()

### Description
Performs the reverse transformation (encoding) using a given codec's `encode` function.

### Signature
`z.encode(codec: ZodCodec, output: any): InputType`

### Parameters
- **codec** (ZodCodec) - The codec to use for encoding.
- **output** (any) - The output value, expected to conform to the codec's output schema.

### Example
```ts
z.encode(stringToDate, new Date("2024-01-15")); // => "2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z"
```

## z.invertCodec()

### Description
Derives a new codec by swapping the input and output schemas, and their respective decode/encode functions, of an existing codec.

### Signature
`z.invertCodec(codec: ZodCodec): ZodCodec`

### Parameters
- **codec** (ZodCodec) - The original codec to invert.

### Example
```ts
const dateToString = z.invertCodec(stringToDate);

z.decode(dateToString, new Date("2024-01-15")); // => string
z.encode(dateToString, "2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z"); // => Date
```
```

--------------------------------

### Basic z.stringbool() Usage

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Demonstrates the default behavior of `z.stringbool()` for converting between boolean and string representations of 'true'/'false'.

```ts
const stringbool = z.stringbool();

stringbool.decode("true");  // => true
stringbool.decode("false"); // => false

stringbool.encode(true);    // => "true"
stringbool.encode(false);   // => "false"
```

--------------------------------

### Use a Record Schema (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/README.md

Demonstrates how to assign and validate values against a defined record schema.

```ts
const userStore: UserStore = {};

userStore["77d2586b-9e8e-4ecf-8b21-ea7e0530eadd"] = {
  name: "Carlotta"
}; // passes

userStore["77d2586b-9e8e-4ecf-8b21-ea7e0530eadd"] = {
  whatever: "Ice cream sundae"
}; // TypeError
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Playground Script

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs-v3/CONTRIBUTING.md

Command to execute `playground.ts` and watch for changes. This is useful for quick experimentation and testing small code snippets.

```sh
yarn play
```

--------------------------------

### Divergence with preprocess(): Static type vs. Runtime

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/wiki/optionality.md

Shows how `z.input` indicates a required field for a schema using `preprocess()`, but `parse` accepts an empty object at runtime, passing `undefined` to the preprocessor function.

```typescript
z.input<typeof z.object({ a: z.preprocess(fn, T) })>
```

```typescript
z.object({ a: z.preprocess(fn, T) }).parse({})
```

--------------------------------

### Handling Absent Keys with Zod Optional, Default, and Preprocess

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/wiki/optionality.md

Demonstrates how to explicitly allow absence, provide default values, or apply preprocessing to optional schema fields in Zod objects, especially for schema kinds that are intentionally strict about absent keys.

```ts
// Want absence to be allowed on coerce? Wrap in optional:
z.object({ a: z.coerce.string().optional() }).parse({})
// → {}

// Want absence to map to a default?
z.object({ a: z.coerce.string().default("x") }).parse({})
// → { a: "x" }

// Want preprocess to fire on absent inner-required schemas? Use inner-optional:
z.object({ a: z.preprocess(fn, z.string().optional()) }).parse({})
// → {}  (or { a: fn(undefined) } if fn returns a defined value — depends on optional)
```

--------------------------------

### Decode and encode with a custom codec

Source: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/blob/main/packages/docs/content/codecs.mdx

Illustrates the distinct behavior of `decode` and `encode` when using a `z.codec()` that transforms between different input and output types.

```ts
stringToDate.decode("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z")
// => Date

stringToDate.encode(new Date("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"))
// => string
```

```ts
z.decode(stringToDate, "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z")
// => Date

z.encode(stringToDate, new Date("2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"))
// => string
```
